en:
Binet [26] says of this term: "Le terme indique la parenté de cet état avec le somnambulisme véritable, et en suite il laisse comprendre que la vie somnambulique qui se manifeste durant la veille est réduite, déprimée, par la conscience normale qui la recouvre."

Automatisms.

Semi-somnambulism is characterised by the continuity of consciousness with that of the waking state and by the appearance of various automatisms which give evidence of an activity of the subconscious self, independent of that of consciousness.

Our case shows the following automatic phenomena:

(1) Automatic movements of the table. (2) Automatic writing. (3) Hallucinations.

(1) Automatic movements of the table.

(2) Automatic writing.

(3) Hallucinations.

1. Automatic Movements of the Table. —Before the patient came under my observation she had been influenced by the suggestion of "table-turning," which she had first come across as a game. As soon as she entered the circle there appeared communications from members of her family which showed her to be a medium. I could only find out that, as soon as ever her hand was placed on the table, the typical movements began. The resulting communications have no interest for us. But the automatic character of the act itself deserves some discussion, for we may, without more ado, set aside the imputation that there was any question of intentional and voluntary pushing or pulling on the part of the patient.

As we know from the investigations of Chevreul, [27] Gley, Lehmann and others, unconscious motor phenomena are not only of frequent occurrence among hysterical persons, and those pathologically inclined in other directions, but they are also relatively easily produced in normal persons who show no other spontaneous automatisms. I have made many experiments on these lines, and can confirm this observation. In the great majority of instances all that is required is enough patience to put up with an hour of quiet waiting. In most subjects, motor automatisms will be obtained in a more or less high degree if contra-suggestions do not intervene as obstacles. In a relatively small percentage the phenomena arise spontaneously, i.e. directly under the influence of verbal suggestion or of some earlier auto-suggestion. In this instance the case is powerfully affected by suggestion. In general, the particular predisposition is subject to all those laws which also hold good for normal hypnosis. Nevertheless, certain special circumstances are to be taken into account, conditioned by the peculiarity of the case. It is not a question of a total hypnosis, but of a partial one, limited entirely to the motor area of the arm, like the cerebral anæsthesia produced by "magnetic passes" for a painful spot in the body. We touch the spot in question employing verbal suggestion or making use of some existing auto-suggestion, using the tactile stimulus which we know acts suggestively, to bring about the desired partial hypnosis. In accordance with this procedure, refractory subjects can be brought easily enough to an exhibition of automatism. The experimenter intentionally gives the table a slight push, or, better, a series of rhythmic but very slight taps. After a short time he notices that the oscillations become stronger, that they continue although he has interrupted his own intentional movements. The experiment has succeeded, the subject has unsuspectingly taken up the suggestion. By this procedure much more is obtained than by verbal suggestion. In very receptive persons and in all those cases where movement seems to arise spontaneously, the purposeful tremulous movements, [28] not perceptible by the subject, assume the rôle of agent provocateur .
zh:
比內[26]如此描述這個術語：「該詞表明此狀態與真正夢遊症的親緣關係，同時也讓人理解到，在清醒期間顯現的夢遊生活，是被覆蓋其上的正常意識所縮減、壓抑的。」

自動現象。

半夢遊症的特徵在於，其意識與清醒狀態的意識具有連續性，並出現各種自動現象，這些現象證明瞭潛意識自我的活動，且此活動獨立於意識之外。

我們的案例顯示了以下自動現象：

(1) 桌子的自動移動。 (2) 自動書寫。 (3) 幻覺。

(1) 桌子的自動移動。

(2) 自動書寫。

(3) 幻覺。

1. 桌子的自動移動。——在患者接受我的觀察之前，她已受到「桌靈轉」暗示的影響，她最初是將此當作遊戲接觸到的。她一加入圈子，便出現了來自其家庭成員的通訊，顯示她是一個靈媒。我只能查明，一旦她的手放在桌上，典型的移動便開始了。所產生的通訊對我們而言並無趣味。但該行為本身的自動特性值得一些討論，因為我們可以毫不猶豫地排除患者有任何故意且自願推或拉桌子的指控。

正如我們從謝弗勒爾[27]、格萊、萊曼等人的研究中所知，無意識的運動現象不僅在歇斯底里患者及其他有病理傾向的人身上頻繁發生，而且在沒有表現出其他自發性自動現象的正常人身上也相對容易產生。我沿著這些方向做了許多實驗，並能證實這項觀察。在絕大多數案例中，所需要的只是足夠的耐心，以忍受一小時的安靜等待。在大多數受試者中，只要反暗示不作為障礙介入，就能獲得程度不等的運動自動現象。在相對較小的百分比中，這些現象是自發出現的，亦即直接受到言語暗示或某些先前自我暗示的影響。在這個案例中，暗示對其產生了強烈的影響。一般而言，特定的易感性服從於所有那些同樣適用於正常催眠的法則。然而，必須考慮到某些特殊情況，這些情況是由該案例的特殊性所決定的。這並非完全的催眠，而是部分的催眠，完全侷限於手臂的運動區域，就像透過「磁力撫摸」為身體的疼痛點所產生的腦部感覺缺失一樣。我們觸及該痛點時，運用了言語暗示或利用了某種現有的自我暗示，使用了我們知道具有暗示作用的觸覺刺激，以引發所需的局部催眠。根據此一程序，抗拒的受試者也能相當容易地被引導至表現出自動現象。實驗者故意輕輕推動桌子，或者更佳的做法是，進行一系列有節奏但極輕微的敲擊。短時間後，他會注意到擺動變得更加強烈，即使他中斷了自己有意識的動作，擺動仍在繼續。實驗成功了，受試者已在不知不覺中接受了暗示。透過此一程序，所獲得的成果遠比言語暗示多得多。在非常易受暗示的人以及所有那些移動似乎自發出現的案例中，那些受試者自身無法察覺的有目的性顫抖運動[28]，扮演了挑釁者的角色。
